qa.question: Define the word “innocence.” Define it as applied to a man and an age.
qa.answer: The word “innocence” as applied to a period of time or a dispensation means that it was an age of sinlessness, innocence, harmlessness, and freedom from guilt or sin on the part of man who was responsible to rule for God in this period. Hence, we conclude that he sinned before Eve conceived and that man was not long in a state of innocence before he ate of the forbidden fruit.

qa.question: Do the Scriptures teach a general resurrection and judgment?
qa.answer: The Scriptures do not teach that there is a general judgment of all the righteous and wicked at the same time and place. There has been and will be seven judgments for angels and men. qa.question: Define “providence.” Do all creatures exercise providence in some measure?
qa.answer: The word “providence” means “foresight and forethought,” “the care of God over His creatures,” “divine superintendence,” or “direction for and over His creation.” Foresight and forethought on the part of anybody imply a future end, a goal, and a definite plan in attaining that end. Although all rational beings exercise providence according to their powers, the Word reaches its full significance only when it is applied to the infinite God.

qa.question: Does the New Testament teach unlimited power for believers? Prove.

qa.question: Define the term “power of attorney.” Who may give it and who may use it?
qa.answer: Authority of an agent may be terminated in several ways: by the express revocation thereof by the principal, by renunciation of such power by the agent, by the death of either the principal or the agent, by the expiration of the time for which the agent was given authority, by the bankruptcy or sale of the business of the principal, and by the insanity of either the principal or the agent.

qa.question: Name the three ways in which one may receive the power of attorney.

qa.question: Are the terms and limitations of power always expressed in legal forms of the power of attorney?
qa.answer: Whenever it becomes necessary to delegate to an agent power to sign notes, checks, or other legal documents, it is advisable to grant such powers in a written document and to have it recorded in court. Such document is usually called the power of attorney. It should set forth exactly and explicitly what the attorney or agent has power to do.

qa.question: Can a man exercise full authority in the place of another to the full extent of the power granted? Can he misuse his power if he desires?
qa.answer: Express authority is given to an agent by what is called the power of attorney. An agent cannot appoint a substitute or delegate power to another without the consent of the principal. The authority of an agent may be constituted in three ways: by deed under seal, by writing, or by mere word. The principal is liable for the acts of agents when they are acting in fulfillment of the agency business, and the agent who conceals his principal is himself responsible to third parties.

qa.question: Define law, moral law, and physical law.
qa.answer: Physical law is the rule of action of the material universe whereby all things are kept perpetually in their own courses, and work in harmony according to the divine plan. Moral law is the rule of action for free moral agents to direct them in their moral action and their own creative powers according to the divine plan. THE EXTENT OF OBEDIENCE TO THE MORAL LAW.

qa.question: Define the Antechaotic Age . How long did it last?
qa.answer: The Antechaotic Age extended from the original creation of the heavens and the earth and all things therein to the rebellion and overthrow of the first kosmos , or social order on the earth. Therefore, we call this period the Antechaotic Age . So we call it a “dispensation,” which means a moral or probationary period in which God tests free moral agents according to a fixed standard of conduct.

qa.question: What does it mean to be a son of God, and what are sons partakers of.
qa.answer: We have repeatedly pointed out that the terms “children of God” and “sons of God” mean something. When we say that God is our Father and that we are His children, we mean that God has not only accepted us but that He has endued us with power to become the sons of God with power to represent Him in the world as He would represent Himself.

qa.question: What has God provided as spiritual tools for His sons?
qa.answer: The spiritual tools of God are the gifts of the Spirit which He wants exercised in full by all of His Earth-sons. God has provided the baptism in the Spirit and the gifts of the Spirit for such manifestation of divine life in His sons. It is likewise contrary to nature that men can be born of God spiritually and partake of His nature and live and act contrary to the nature of God.

qa.question: Should sons of God be ignorant of their tools and their calling to use them? Why?
qa.answer: The only reason that anyone could possibly misunderstand is that he deliberately refuses to understand, as plainly expressed by the statement above, “But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.” There is nothing more specific and detailed in Scripture than the plan of God for divine-human sons endued with power to represent God in the Earth. It seems that the more simple and detailed the truths of God are, the more people are ignorant of them.

qa.question: Were gifts of the Spirit manifested before Christ? Which ones?
qa.answer: In Lesson Eighteen, Point IX, we have seen that the gifts of the Spirit were manifest in all Old Testament ages with the exception of the gift of tongues and interpretation of tongues. qa.answer: Saints of old had great power with God and even greater power in many instances than manifested today through modern believers. qa.question: Define the word “kingdom.”
qa.answer: The word “Heaven” in all the above passages is in the plural, and the phrase “kingdom of Heaven” literally means “the kingdom from the heavens.” It is a dispensational term and refers to the Messiah’s kingdom on the Earth. There can be no kingdom apart from a king. While it is true that without a king a country remains the same, has the same people, the same cities, the same mountains and rivers, etc., it is no longer a kingdom.

qa.question: Define the terms “kingdom of Heaven” and “kingdom of God.”
qa.answer: The usage of the two terms in parallel passages may be explained as follows: The kingdom of Heaven is a lesser term than the kingdom of God. It is the earthly sphere of the universal kingdom of God, and in this respect the terms have almost all things in common. On the other hand, there are some statements made of the kingdom of God in this age that could not possibly be spoken of the kingdom of Heaven.

qa.question: Give plenty of biblical proof that the Old Covenant including the Ten Commandments was entirely “abolished” and “done away” to make way for the New Covenant.
qa.answer: The whole law of God and Moses, including the Ten Commandments, and all the civil and ceremonial laws based upon the Ten Commandments, were done away with and abolished in Christ on the cross and the New Covenant now takes the place of the old one. Some people may need an interpreter to explain this simple passage, but to the common reader it is clear that the Old Covenant has been “done away” and “abolished” entirely and the New Covenant has taken its place.

qa.question: Discuss the fact and usage of sanctification in Scripture.

cmnknk48j001mewhjkkrwuhn8.title: Lesson 35 — The Truth About Eternal Security

qa.question: State fully the question involved in the teaching of unconditional eternal security.
qa.answer: What is the Question Involved in the Teaching of Unconditional Security? What is the Question Involved in the Teaching of Unconditional Security?

qa.question: Does the Bible teach conditional eternal security?
qa.answer: The Bible does teach conditional eternal security, which is the only kind of security a sane man can expect and a just God can tolerate and promise. But to teach an unconditional eternal security and maintain this position in the face of overwhelming scriptural authority to the contrary is to make God the most unjust tyrant of the universe. One must twist, contort, misinterpret, misapply, take from, and add to many Scriptures to support such teaching.

qa.question: Upon what principles should every Bible doctrine be based?
qa.answer: Every Bible doctrine should be measured by plain facts of Scripture and by plain principles and true examples of life; not by erroneously interpreting a few texts in total disregard of other Scriptures on the same subject. Any doctrine that is opposed to the true principles of life, or to one plain Scripture, rightly divided, is bound to be wrong.

qa.question: How do men get around certain scriptures that condemn unconditional security?
qa.answer: Regrettably, for too many people the teaching of eternal security becomes eternal insecurity for they lapse into a sinning-saint religion, which will truly bring them disappointments on Judgment Day. Some people accept the arguments because of ignorance of the full scope of Scriptures on the subject, while there are no doubt others who, being willfully blind, praise it because they want an excuse to sin.

qa.question: Is it possible to prove any doctrine from one Scripture? Why?
qa.answer: Some people accept the arguments because of ignorance of the full scope of Scriptures on the subject, while there are no doubt others who, being willfully blind, praise it because they want an excuse to sin. That there is a true sense in which every believer can have “everlasting life,” and be “eternally secure” cannot be denied—for they are possible whenever God’s conditions are met.

qa.question: How many Scriptures are required to prove any doctrine?
qa.answer: Every Bible doctrine should be measured by plain facts of Scripture and by plain principles and true examples of life; not by erroneously interpreting a few texts in total disregard of other Scriptures on the same subject. To do so, one must manufacture and maintain many other false doctrines to support the sinking-sand foundation of the main false doctrine. One must twist, contort, misinterpret, misapply, take from, and add to many Scriptures to support such teaching.

qa.question: What should be done if two or three Scriptures can be interpreted to teach a doctrine that is in conflict with other Scriptures?
qa.answer: Every Bible doctrine should be measured by plain facts of Scripture and by plain principles and true examples of life; not by erroneously interpreting a few texts in total disregard of other Scriptures on the same subject. One must twist, contort, misinterpret, misapply, take from, and add to many Scriptures to support such teaching. To do so, one must manufacture and maintain many other false doctrines to support the sinking-sand foundation of the main false doctrine.

qa.question: Should we teach any theory that is in conflict with the perfect harmony of all Scripture?
qa.answer: Teachers of unconditional security, like teachers of other false doctrines, do away with all Scriptures that are not in harmony with their pet theory. To teach unconditional security is to leave an irreparable breach between two sets of Scriptures, but to teach conditional security is to have perfect harmony between all Scriptures. On the other hand, these many hundreds of passages cannot possibly be harmonized with the wrong interpretations of the few that are used to teach unconditional security.

qa.question: What false doctrines do we have to contend for in order to teach unconditional security?
qa.answer: Teachers of unconditional security, like teachers of other false doctrines, do away with all Scriptures that are not in harmony with their pet theory. But to teach an unconditional eternal security and maintain this position in the face of overwhelming scriptural authority to the contrary is to make God the most unjust tyrant of the universe. The Bible does teach conditional eternal security, which is the only kind of security a sane man can expect and a just God can tolerate and promise.

qa.question: Define eternal life. Whose life is it? Do we get it until we get in God? Does it make us self-existent? Is it our life apart from God? Why?
qa.answer: That there is a true sense in which every believer can have “everlasting life,” and be “eternally secure” cannot be denied—for they are possible whenever God’s conditions are met. It is vital for one to get God’s thought on the subject as a whole as expressed in all Scripture. Any doctrine that is opposed to the true principles of life, or to one plain Scripture, rightly divided, is bound to be wrong.

qa.question: Does eternal life make us a part of God? Why?
qa.answer: That there is a true sense in which every believer can have “everlasting life,” and be “eternally secure” cannot be denied—for they are possible whenever God’s conditions are met. But to teach an unconditional eternal security and maintain this position in the face of overwhelming scriptural authority to the contrary is to make God the most unjust tyrant of the universe.

qa.question: Can a person lose fellowship and not relationship?
qa.answer: Under the guise of honoring the finished work of Christ on the cross, some teachers lead many to believe that once a person is in grace, he will always be in grace, regardless of how much he sins thereafter. Such teachings masquerade under various names, and are believed by a great many people because they seem plausible; and furthermore they are sometimes championed by Bible scholars of national or worldwide reputation.

qa.question: Has God ever sent any of His sons to Hell? Is He yet going to do so? Then what good is relationship when fellowship is lost?
qa.answer: Yet it is argued that man can separate himself from God . To say that a man can will to go away from God and be lost is to make the sovereign grace of God subject to the will of man.” Under the guise of honoring the finished work of Christ on the cross, some teachers lead many to believe that once a person is in grace, he will always be in grace, regardless of how much he sins thereafter.

qa.question: Can any plain Scripture or concrete example be given to prove that fellowship with God can be lost and that God will still take the disfellowshipped ones to Heaven?
qa.answer: To say that a man can will to go away from God and be lost is to make the sovereign grace of God subject to the will of man.” Every Bible doctrine should be measured by plain facts of Scripture and by plain principles and true examples of life; not by erroneously interpreting a few texts in total disregard of other Scriptures on the same subject.
